diary:
Sunny Monday, and I could feel it in my shoulders right when I woke up in Bothell. Coffee first, then a quick kitchen reset—because if the sink’s chaotic, my brain gets loud. I checked the rooftop herb garden like I always do; the little plants looked perky, which felt like permission to be perky too. 

I drove into Overlake and spent the morning waiting tables, updating my set notes in the little gaps between refills. Between guests, I scribbled ideas that sounded like jokes but probably weren’t funny until I said them out loud. Around lunchtime I ducked to Dough Zone on Capitol Hill and caught the USA vs Wales match at a downtown bar—cheering felt oddly energizing, like the city was on my side. 

The light rail being closed between Westlake and University District made the afternoon commute weirdly quiet, but I kept moving. After work I ran the river paths near Juanita Bay Park, then grabbed takeout at Whole Foods Market (Roosevelt). Tonight I’m knitting again, and my herbs are still thriving. I feel steady—like I earned the calm.
thought_process: I planned the day around small anchors: mornings for work flow, midday for a controlled burst of fun, and late afternoon for movement. The light rail closure nudged me to rely on driving and walking without overthinking it, so I didn’t let transit uncertainty sour my rhythm. The World Cup match mattered more than I expected; watching it at Dough Zone downtown gave me a shared-laughter feeling I could carry back into the waiting shift—especially during quieter lulls. My mood shifted like a tide: focused and a little tense in the morning (always checking my notes and keeping tables smooth), then brighter around the match, then grounded again after the trail run. I chose affordable dinner ingredients because I’m saving for future set gear, but also because comfort matters after a long service day.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
